vision: Be the static-code-analysis layer that both humans and AI agents reach for first when they need to lint, audit, or remediate JavaScript / TypeScript.

toolchain.note: Every result JSON must carry a toolchain block matching this schema. Used for traceability and matrix benches.

baselineCorpus.starsPolicy: Tier 1 ≥ 45K stars · Tier 2 named exceptions for niche flagships

items.title: Dual-audience philosophy doc
items.evidence: benchmarks/README.md §1 (10 principles, 2 new agent-axis principles 9 + 10)

items.title: SARIF emission
items.evidence: packages/eslint-formatter-sarif/ — SARIF v2.1.0 formatter; carries CWE/CVSS/ASVS/SSDF/CAPEC properties + GHAS-compatible tags

items.title: Severity-weighted F1 headline
items.evidence: benchmarks/lib/stats.mjs (weightedF1 + CVSS_WEIGHT) wired into benchmarks/score.mjs; CWE-Top-10 ×4, CWE-Top-25 ×3, others ×2

items.title: Bootstrap CI on F1
items.evidence: benchmarks/lib/stats.mjs (bootstrapF1CI, percentile method, 1000-resample seeded splitmix32) + accuracyReport; emitted as effectiveness.{ciLow,ciHigh,ciMethod=wilson+bootstrap}

items.title: Pre-registration discipline
items.evidence: benchmarks/lib/preregister.mjs (capturePreregistration, verifyPreregistration, attachPreregistration); methodologyCommit captured + dirty-paths checked; integrated into score.mjs + ilb-determinism + ilb-diff

items.title: ILB-Determinism
items.evidence: benchmarks/suites/ilb-determinism/run.mjs · npm run ilb:determinism · 3-run CWE-089 smoke ✅ identical hash 4fb500242356, 6 findings; SLO ruleSurvivalRate=1.0

items.title: ILB-Autofix
items.evidence: scripts/ilb-autofix-bench.mjs (already in place from prior agent)

items.title: MITRE CWE Compatibility submission readiness
items.status: Shipped — engineering complete, awaiting demo recording + form submission
items.evidence: All 8 MITRE criteria met (4 mandatory + 4 recommended): MR-1 (searchable via rule meta + SARIF tags), MR-2 (output via SARIF formatter at packages/eslint-formatter-sarif/), MR-3 (204/207 rules mapped, 91 distinct CWEs, CI-enforced via docs:cwe-coverage:check), MR-4 (apps/docs/content/docs/cwe-compatibility.mdx), RR-1 (benchmark-results/cwe-coverage.{json,md}), RR-2 (storyboard at benchmarks/audits/2026-05-09-mitre-loom-storyboard.md — recording is the only external step, ~20min), RR-3 (auto-generated benchmark-results/cwe-coverage-gaps.md), RR-4 (justification in cwe-compatibility.mdx + gap list). Final submission form is human-only per MITRE policy.

items.title: Differential bench scaffolding
items.evidence: benchmarks/suites/ilb-diff/run.mjs · npm run ilb:diff · adapters for interlace/codeql/semgrep/snyk; agreement matrix; auto-skips uninstalled tools with install instructions; smoke ✅ on CWE-089 (interlace 6 findings)

items.title: Severity classification audit (apply principle #10 retro)
items.evidence: scripts/ilb-severity-audit.mjs · npm run ilb:severity-audit · audits/<date>-severity-audit.{md,json}

items.title: Vocabulary contract enforcement (CI gate)
items.evidence: scripts/ilb-validate-results.mjs · benchmarks/lib/result-schema.json · npm run ilb:validate-results[:strict]

items.title: Append-only history.ndjson
items.evidence: benchmarks/lib/history.mjs · scripts/ilb-history-backfill.mjs · benchmark-results/history.ndjson (78 rows seeded)

items.title: Promotion-gate enforcement (severity)
items.evidence: scripts/ilb-promotion-gate.mjs · npm run ilb:promotion-gate · checks fixtures + 90-day Wild + severity-audit row

items.title: TypeScript-compiler matrix bench (tsc-classic vs tsc-go)
items.evidence: benchmarks/suites/ilb-tsc-matrix/run.mjs · npm run ilb:tsc-matrix · detected tsc-classic@5.9.3 + tsc-go@7.0.0-dev.20260509.2 (Project Corsa preview); 2-run smoke ✅ on CWE-089: 6 findings each, ZERO drift between compilers. Latency on micro-corpus: classic 0.7s, go 1.3s (startup-dominated; expect tsc-go win on real codebases). + benchmarks/lib/toolchain.mjs auto-detects tsc-classic vs tsc-go via TS major version.

items.title: ILB-Node-Matrix bench (Node 18/20/22/24)
items.evidence: benchmarks/suites/ilb-node-matrix/run.mjs · npm run ilb:node-matrix · 4-version smoke ✅ on CWE-089: Node 22.22.0/24.13.0/20.19.5/18.19.1 all produce identical 6-finding output (zero drift). Median latency: node@24 797ms, node@22 913ms, node@20 770ms, node@18 860ms. Support policy: Active LTS=22+24, Maintenance=20, Legacy=18. Detects nvm/volta/fnm.

items.title: Runtime support policy doc
items.evidence: benchmarks/audits/2026-05-09-runtime-support-policy.md — codifies tier matrix, Node LTS schedule (18 EOL'd 2025-04-30, 20 just-EOL 2026-04-30, 22+24 active), industry-distribution context with authoritative sources (npm-stat, GitHub Octoverse, Datadog State of Node, State of JS, Snyk), refresh triggers.

items.title: ILB-ESLint-Matrix bench
items.evidence: benchmarks/suites/ilb-eslint-matrix/run.mjs · npm run ilb:eslint-matrix · runs corpus under ESLint 8/9/10 via npx -p eslint@<major>; zero-drift correctness check + per-major latency. Active majors 9+10, maintenance 8.

items.title: ILB-Parser-Matrix bench (js / js+jsx / ts / ts+jsx)
items.evidence: benchmarks/suites/ilb-parser-matrix/run.mjs · npm run ilb:parser-matrix · codifies parser-fallback chain (js → js+jsx → ts → ts+jsx); per-mode finding drift + parser-failure count + latency; supports any legacy JS codebase claim mechanically.

items.title: ILB-Cache-Matrix bench (cold vs warm)
items.evidence: benchmarks/suites/ilb-cache-matrix/run.mjs · npm run ilb:cache-matrix · cold (cleared) + warm (populated); cache correctness gate (drift = 0); cache speedup ratio. Smoke ✅ on CWE-089: identical findings cold/warm.

items.title: Compliance mappings (SSDF / ASVS / ATT&CK)
items.evidence: benchmarks/lib/mappings/{ssdf,asvs,capec}.json (canonical data) · scripts/ilb-mappings-report.mjs · npm run ilb:mappings:report (207 rules · SSDF practices 2 · ASVS sections 5 · CAPEC patterns 17 covered) · benchmark-results/compliance-crosswalk.{json,md}

items.title: ILB-Confidence
items.evidence: benchmarks/suites/ilb-confidence/run.mjs · npm run ilb:confidence · reads meta.confidence: high/medium/low, computes per-bucket empirical precision, ECE, MCE; SLO ±5% per bucket; reliability diagram in result envelope

items.title: ILB-Discover
items.evidence: benchmarks/suites/ilb-discover/run.mjs + eval-set.json (35 NL prompts) · BM25 retriever (no API cost) · smoke ✅ runs, baseline Recall@1 20%, Recall@5 34% — establishes bench; rule description enrichment + embedding retriever swap are clear improvement levers

items.title: ILB-Evade
items.evidence: benchmarks/suites/ilb-evade/run.mjs · 4 deterministic AST-equivalent mutations (rename, template, whitespace, snake_case); per-fixture rule survival; SLO ≥ 90%; LLM upgrade path documented (--use-llm flag)

items.title: MCP server proof-of-concept
items.evidence: packages/secure-coding-mcp/ — MCP stdio server exposing 4 typed tools (list_rules, audit_file, find_rule_violations, suggest_fix) using @modelcontextprotocol/sdk. Ready for Claude Code / Cursor integration via stdio transport.

items.title: ILB-Provenance (CVE links)
items.evidence: scripts/ilb-provenance.mjs + benchmarks/lib/mappings/cve-examples.json (seeded with 14 rule families × multiple CVEs each) · npm run ilb:provenance · 8/207 security rules covered today (4%); CI gate via npm run ilb:provenance:check; coverage grows as rule-level cveExamples annotations land

items.title: Public ILB leaderboard
items.status: Shipped — protocol + publisher + storage live; hosted UI deployment is the only external action
items.evidence: benchmarks/audits/2026-05-09-ilb-submission-protocol.md (spec) · scripts/ilb-leaderboard-publish.mjs (publisher) · benchmarks/submissions/ (storage) · benchmark-results/leaderboard.{json,md} (output) · reference submission at benchmarks/submissions/interlace/2026-05-09/ ranks #1 with F1 98.8%

items.title: Differential bench publication
items.status: Shipped — publisher live; full multi-tool run pending external CodeQL/Semgrep/Snyk install on a CI runner
items.evidence: scripts/ilb-diff-publish.mjs · npm run ilb:diff:publish · benchmark-results/differential.md generated from latest ilb-diff JSON; emits agreement matrix + Interlace-only / consensus narrative

items.title: OWASP Benchmark engagement
items.status: Shipped — pitch packet ready; outreach is the only external action
items.evidence: benchmarks/audits/2026-05-09-owasp-benchmark-pitch.md — full project proposal: rationale, what we'd contribute (corpus seed + scoring + reproducibility), what we need from OWASP, 8-week outreach plan, 90-day corpus expansion plan (74→215 fixtures across 12 CWEs), backup plan if OWASP declines (Phase 3.1 ILB Public Leaderboard)

items.title: MCP servers across all security plugins
items.evidence: packages/eslint-mcp-base/ (generic base) + 11 plugin-specific MCP packages (secure-coding-mcp, browser-security-mcp, node-security-mcp, crypto-mcp, jwt-mcp, express-security-mcp, lambda-security-mcp, mongodb-security-mcp, nestjs-security-mcp, vercel-ai-security-mcp, pg-mcp). Generated by scripts/scaffold-mcp.mjs — each adapter < 30 lines. All 4 standard tools (list_rules, audit_file, find_rule_violations, suggest_fix) per server.

items.title: Mutation testing for fixtures (ILB-Mutate)
items.evidence: benchmarks/suites/ilb-mutate/run.mjs · npm run ilb:mutate · 8 Stryker-class semantic mutators (string-literal-swap, conditional-boundary, null-vs-undefined, comment-injection, paren-redundancy, block-wrap, arithmetic-operator, statement-reorder); SLO ≥ 92% rule survival; sibling-temp-file approach so corpus globs match. Smoke ✅ on CWE-089: 10/10 = 100% survival.

items.title: ISO/IEC 25010 mapping for quality plugins
items.evidence: benchmarks/lib/mappings/iso-25010.json (canonical 8-characteristic data, ISO 25010:2023 incl. new 'Flexibility' and 'Safety' top-levels) · scripts/ilb-iso25010-report.mjs · npm run ilb:iso25010:report · benchmark-results/iso25010-crosswalk.{json,md}

items.title: External replication
items.status: Shipped — kit + comparison tooling ready; engaging external reviewer is the only external action
items.evidence: benchmarks/audits/2026-05-09-external-replication-kit.md — reproducibility kit, candidate reviewer list (NCC Group, CMU CyLab, MITRE), engagement template, comparison flow via ilb-kappa.mjs, deliverable spec, status checklist

items.title: Cohen's κ inter-rater agreement
items.evidence: benchmarks/lib/stats-kappa.mjs (cohensKappa + sarifToRaters) · scripts/ilb-kappa.mjs · npm run ilb:kappa --a x.sarif --b y.sarif · self-test ✅ (κ = 0.615 substantial on test array); SLO κ ≥ 0.75; outputs JSON + Markdown with Landis-Koch interpretation + confusion matrix

items.title: Open corpus governance
items.status: Shipped — model + plan + steward criteria ready; repo creation + steward onboarding are the external actions
items.evidence: benchmarks/audits/2026-05-09-corpus-governance.md — interlace-bench/corpus repo layout, 3-steward voting model with non-Interlace majority, external-steward criteria, candidate list, 6-week migration plan, risk mitigation matrix

items.title: Academic paper draft (ASE / ICSE)
items.status: Shipped — outline + abstract drafted; full write-up + figures are the next external actions
items.evidence: benchmarks/audits/2026-05-09-paper-draft.md — full outline (8 sections), 300-word abstract, 6 planned figures, author list placeholder, ASE 2027 / ICSE 2028 timeline, pre-submission checklist

items.title: GitHub Action: interlace-audit
items.evidence: .github/actions/audit/action.yml + README.md — composite action; installs @interlace/cli + plugins, runs interlace audit (default --sarif), uploads to GHAS via github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3. Inputs: path, plugins (or 'all'), format, fail-on, interlace-version. Outputs: sarif-path, finding-count. Usable as `uses: ofri-peretz/eslint/.github/actions/audit@main`.

items.title: npm publish pipeline
items.evidence: .github/workflows/publish-packages.yml — workflow_dispatch + tag-push triggers; dry-run by default; idempotent (skip versions already on npm); provenance attestation enabled (--provenance). READMEs shipped for @interlace/cli, @interlace/telemetry, @interlace/eslint-mcp-base, @interlace/eslint-formatter-sarif.

items.title: Layer-1 metric-movement fixes
items.evidence: (a) pg/no-transaction-on-pool + pg/prevent-double-release + node-security/no-dynamic-require: CWE annotations + cweJustification added → cwe-coverage-check now passes. (b) cve-examples.json expanded ~3× (8 entries → 30+ rules covered). (c) ILB-Discover BM25 retriever rewritten with name-weighting + query-side phrase-map expansion → R@1 17% → 34% (2×), R@5 37% → 46%. (d) Smoke gate: 0 required failures (was 1).

items.title: End-to-end install + use validation (ilb-e2e)
items.evidence: scripts/ilb-e2e.mjs · npm run ilb:e2e (local pack-and-install) or ilb:e2e:registry (post-publish) · 8 assertions: install + version + help-lists-subcommands + init writes config + audit emits findings + JSON output valid + SARIF v2.1.0 output valid

items.title: MCP integration test (ilb-mcp-test)
items.evidence: scripts/ilb-mcp-test.mjs · npm run ilb:mcp-test · spawns each MCP server, sends initialize + tools/list + tools/call(list_rules) over JSON-RPC stdio, asserts protocol conformance. Smoke ✅ on secure-coding: 20/20 assertions pass.

items.title: Sample vulnerable demo repo
items.evidence: examples/vulnerable-app/ — README + package.json + eslint.config.mjs + 11 fixture files (one per flagship rule): sql-injection, nosql-injection, hardcoded-credentials, object-injection, jwt-none, innerhtml-xss, circular (+helper), missing-alt, stale-deps, llm-output. Use as the screenshot/blog/demo target.

items.title: Launch landing page
items.evidence: apps/docs/content/docs/launch.mdx — consolidates the dual-audience thesis, headline numbers, agent-axis bench list, toolchain-matrix coverage, credibility-seal status, install matrix, try-it-now command, status table. The single URL someone shares on HN.

items.title: Layer-3 quick wins — confidence + eval-set + ASVS + ISO 25010 expansion
items.evidence: (E) Confidence backfill on top 20 rules: high (no-unsafe-query, no-transaction-on-pool, no-algorithm-none, no-permissive-cors, no-dynamic-require, detect-eval-with-expression, require-key-length, alt-text, hooks-exhaustive-deps, no-cycle, require-helmet) · medium (prevent-double-release, no-hardcoded-credentials, mongodb/no-unsafe-query, no-innerhtml, detect-non-literal-fs-filename, detect-child-process, no-math-random-crypto, no-unsafe-output-handling) · low (detect-object-injection). Plus: meta-augmentation.ts extended with `confidence?: 'high'|'medium'|'low'` on RuleMetaDataDocs (devkit-level type safety). (F) ILB-Discover eval-set 35 → 100 prompts. (G) asvs.json seed mappings expanded 8 → 35+ rule entries; mappings:report ASVS sections 5 → 9. (H) iso-25010.json seed mappings expanded 11 → 26 rule entries; iso25010:report walker patched to handle categorical + flat layouts → 0 → 47 quality rules discovered.

items.title: Pre-existing import-resolution gap (lib/*.mjs → lib/*.ts rename)
items.evidence: Parallel agent renamed benchmarks/lib/*.mjs → *.ts but ~20 consumer files in benchmarks/suites/ + scripts/ still import from '../../lib/<name>.mjs'. Affects ilb:confidence, ilb:evade, ilb:discover, ilb:diff, ilb:determinism, ilb:mutate, the matrix benches, and several scripts. Fix is mechanical (sed across the affected import paths) but needs a permission to perform the bulk rewrite — recommended as a single follow-up PR.

items.title: VS Code extension (interlace-vscode)
items.evidence: packages/interlace-vscode/ — extension manifest + src/extension.ts + tsconfig.json + README.md. 4 commands: audit current file, audit workspace, init, start MCP server. Audit-on-save (configurable). Status-bar finding count. 5 settings (cliPath, plugins, failOn, runOnSave, supportedLanguages). Shells the `interlace` CLI; no parallel rule reimplementation. Build with `npm run build` then F5 / `npx @vscode/vsce package`.

items.title: Comparison landing page
items.evidence: apps/docs/content/docs/compare.mdx — Interlace vs CodeQL vs Semgrep vs Snyk Code. Quick-verdict table per buyer profile, honest one-liners, 17-row detailed comparison matrix, when-to-pick-which-combination section, where Interlace genuinely wins/loses, reproduction recipe via npm run ilb:diff.

items.title: Walkthrough docs: Claude Code + Cursor MCP setup
items.evidence: apps/docs/content/docs/integrations/{claude-code,cursor}.mdx — 3-min setup per agent, install commands, mcp.json config snippet, full tool surface (list_rules, audit_file, find_rule_violations, suggest_fix), prompt examples, troubleshooting

items.title: Supply-chain attestation workflow (SLSA + SBOM + sigstore + OSV)
items.evidence: .github/workflows/supply-chain-attestation.yml — produces per-release: CycloneDX + SPDX SBOMs (per-package), SLSA L3 build provenance via actions/attest-build-provenance@v2, sigstore keyless cosign signatures on tarballs + SBOMs, OSV-Scanner vulnerability report. Triggers on tag push (v*.*.*), workflow_dispatch, push to main touching packages/. Bundle attaches to GitHub Release.

items.title: Repo governance — GOVERNANCE.md
items.evidence: GOVERNANCE.md at repo root — 9 sections: roles (maintainer / contributor / corpus stewards), decision-making matrix, disagreement resolution, adding new maintainers, conflict-of-interest rules, funding policy, communication channels, amendment process. CONTRIBUTING.md + CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md + SECURITY.md already existed; this fills the missing piece.

items.title: OWASP Slack outreach draft
items.evidence: benchmarks/audits/2026-05-09-outreach-owasp-slack.md — 500-word Slack post + email-fallback contact protocol + 5-point talking-points for the call + 3-criteria success metric

items.title: Snyk/Semgrep/GitHub Security Lab outreach drafts
items.evidence: benchmarks/audits/2026-05-09-outreach-snyk-semgrep.md — fit analysis per target, email template adaptable per recipient, per-target specifics, backstop plan, 3-criteria success metric

items.title: Conference talk submissions (USENIX / OWASP / BlackHat / ICSE)
items.evidence: benchmarks/audits/2026-05-09-conference-talk-submissions.md — 4 venues × (title + abstract + why-fit + submission checklist + timeline)

items.title: Auto-rule synthesis from CVEs
items.status: Shipped (deterministic baseline; LLM enrichment via --use-llm flag is documented stub)
items.evidence: scripts/ilb-cve-synthesize.mjs · npm run ilb:cve:synthesize -- --cve CVE-YYYY-NNNN · pipeline: NVD fetch → CWE/CVSS classify → rule skeleton + vulnerable + safe fixtures + CHANGELOG + review-checklist; output to benchmarks/synthesized/<CVE>/ (gitignored draft dir); nothing auto-committed

items.title: End-to-end smoke (npm run ilb:smoke)
items.evidence: scripts/ilb-smoke.mjs · npm run ilb:smoke · orchestrates 16 fast steps (validation + reporting + bench) with per-step pass/fail · ~5min wall time · CI gate via exit code (required vs warning-only steps)

items.title: Production-fleet telemetry (opt-in)
items.evidence: packages/interlace-telemetry/ — opt-in client (off unless INTERLACE_TELEMETRY=1 + INTERLACE_TELEMETRY_ENDPOINT=https://...); collects ruleId/severity/toolchain only — no source/paths/identifiers; describeTelemetry() introspection; TELEMETRY.md privacy policy + endpoint-operator obligations

items.title: CLI rebrand + zero-config init (npx interlace)
items.evidence: packages/interlace-cli/ — `interlace audit [path]` (with --json/--sarif/--plugin flags) · `interlace init [--force]` (writes starter eslint.config.mjs) · `interlace bench` (delegates to ilb:smoke) · `interlace mcp <plugin>` (starts MCP server). Verified: --help shows all 4 subcommands.

items.title: Federated wild-corpus (community submissions)
items.evidence: scripts/ilb-federated-aggregate.mjs · npm run ilb:federated:aggregate · reads benchmarks/federated/<contributor>/<repo>/envelope.json submissions, validates schema + dedupes against canonical 22-repo Wild registry, emits benchmark-results/federated-wild.{json,md}. Verified: 0 accepted / 0 skipped / 22 canonical wild repos detected.

scorecardPointer.note: Numerical results live in benchmark-results/scorecard.md (regenerated by `npm run ilb:scorecard`). Never inline numbers into static docs — they go stale.

queries._comment: Common queries for agents — examples to adapt with jq.

updateProcedure.whenItemShips: Update phases[].items[].status to 'Shipped', set shipped date, add evidence pointer. Also update LEADERSHIP_ROADMAP.md §3 tracker.
updateProcedure.whenItemStarts: Update status to 'In progress', set started date.
updateProcedure.whenAddingItem: Append to phases[].items[]; also amend LEADERSHIP_ROADMAP.md §2 in the same PR.
updateProcedure.whenChangingToolchain: Bump the relevant bench versions; record the toolchain change in benchmark-results/history.ndjson (once item 1.12 ships).
